title: PTD Today — Media

items.title: Restrukturyzacja a podatki. Rozpoznanie, obowiązki, konsekwencje | Monika Palmowska, Tomasz Regulski

ai.summary: Based on the available description, eksperci KPMG omawiają, jak rozpoznać restrukturyzację z perspektywy cen transferowych i wycen, jakie obowiązki raportowe (m.in. TPR, MDR) mogą się pojawić oraz dlaczego terminowa dokumentacja jest kluczowa. Podcast zawiera przykłady praktyczne (przeniesienie produkcji/dystrybucji, transfer pracowników, przeniesienie kontraktów), omówienie typowych błędów wycen oraz check-listę działań dla firm.
bullets: Definicja restrukturyzacji w kontekście cen transferowych i podejście do rozpoznawania istotnych zmian (analiza EBIT).
Znaczenie przygotowania dokumentacji restrukturyzacji „na czas” dla ograniczenia ryzyka w przyszłości.
Obowiązki raportowe związane ze zmianami operacyjnymi, w tym TPR i MDR.
Rola wycen w procesie restrukturyzacji oraz najczęściej popełniane błędy.
Praktyczne scenariusze: przeniesienie produkcji lub dystrybucji, transfer pracowników, przeniesienie kontraktów i związane z tym pułapki.
takeaways: Przy przenoszeniu operacji (np. produkcja, dystrybucja) ocenić, czy zmiana kwalifikuje się jako restrukturyzacja z punktu widzenia cen transferowych i jakie będzie miała skutki dla EBIT.
Przy planowaniu migracji centrów danych lub zasobów IT zadbać o kompletną dokumentację restrukturyzacyjną zawczasu, by zminimalizować przyszłe spory i kontrole.
Upewnić się, że obowiązki raportowe (TPR, MDR) są rozpoznane i wypełnione przy zmianach transgranicznych lub przy transferze kontraktów/zespołów.
Włączyć wyceny do procesu decyzyjnego i skonsultować się z ekspertami, aby uniknąć typowych błędów wyceny przy przenoszeniu aktywów lub działalności.
Korzystać z przygotowanej check-listy działań, aby uporządkować kroki i wymogi compliance przed wdrożeniem zmian operacyjnych.

bullets: Title frames Copilot as coming through when goals need backup.
Call to action: download and try Copilot via the link in the bio.
Published by the Microsoft Copilot channel on 2026-02-03.
No transcript or additional details are provided in the description.
takeaways: Download the trial to perform a hands-on evaluation of Copilot for your operations.
Use a short pilot to determine whether Copilot aligns with your operational objectives and workflows.
Assess security, compliance, and integration requirements before wider adoption.
Define clear success criteria for any trial so you can measure fit and value for data center/grid use cases.

items.title: The Most Expensive Mistake in AI (And How to Avoid It)

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Marcus Oliver (lead of Google delta) says the most expensive mistake in AI is rushing to code without a clear strategy rather than overspending on compute. He recommends reimagining value chains and deploying multi-agent systems so AI initiatives deliver real business outcomes.
bullets: The key mistake: rushing to code without a strategy — not compute costs.
Marcus Oliver represents Google delta, Google Cloud’s professional services team.
Escape the “coding trap” by focusing on strategy and value identification.
Reimagine value chains to find where AI creates business impact.
Deploy multi-agent systems to drive measurable business outcomes.
takeaways: Prioritize strategy and concrete use cases before investing in compute capacity to avoid wasted infrastructure spend.
Align AI projects with reimagined value chains to ensure data center and grid investments support measurable outcomes.
Engage professional services (e.g., Google delta) to help translate strategy into deployable systems and reduce costly missteps.
Evaluate multi-agent system architectures for operational automations that could change capacity and orchestration needs.

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This replay episode from PwC’s Reporting reset series reviews foundational financial statement presentation topics, including GAAP/SEC requirements, reporting periods, balance sheet and income statement presentation, accounting changes/error corrections, and subsequent events. The episode also provides timestamps for each topic and links to PwC guidance chapters for deeper reading.
bullets: Overview of foundational GAAP and SEC presentation requirements and key disclosure considerations.
Guidance on determining appropriate reporting periods and related presentation timing.
Detailed discussion of balance sheet presentation: classification, required disclosures, and best practices.
Income statement presentation: structure and main considerations for presenting results.
Treatment and disclosure of accounting changes, estimates, error corrections, and subsequent events.
takeaways: Confirm balance sheet classification and disclosure practices are robust—they directly affect how infrastructure-related assets and liabilities are presented to stakeholders.
Align internal reporting and project timelines with appropriate reporting periods to ensure consistent period-to-period comparability.
Review income statement presentation conventions to ensure operating results and cost classifications reflect management and investor needs.
Maintain clear processes to identify, document, and disclose accounting changes, error corrections, and subsequent events in a timely manner.

items.title: DeepKeep: Secure your AI agents and applications end-to-end

ai.summary: Based on the available description, DeepKeep on Google Cloud is a unified platform for securing AI agents, applications, and user interactions, aiming to eliminate shadow AI and protect infrastructure from evolving threats. The offering highlights AI Lens for visibility and policy enforcement, AI Red Teaming for stress-testing models and apps, and an AI Firewall to apply real-time guardrails to prompts and responses.
bullets: Unified platform to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions
AI Lens: visibility and policy application across AI interactions
AI Red Teaming: stress-tests apps and models against real-world attacks
AI Firewall: real-time prompt and response protection with guardrails
Designed to eliminate shadow AI and protect infrastructure from evolving threats
takeaways: Centralized visibility helps detect and remove unsanctioned AI use, lowering operational risk
Regular red teaming provides defensive confidence by exposing real-world attack vectors before they hit production
Real-time prompt/response guardrails reduce the chance of harmful or noncompliant outputs reaching users
A unified security approach can simplify AI governance and help protect critical infrastructure from evolving threats

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Congrats to r/buildapc contest winner u/JeffersonPutnam — GeForce Garage showcases their small-form-factor build centered on a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition. The video notes the system specs and host Dwight, and links to a full PCPartPicker parts list.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam; build is a SFF GeForce RTX 5090 rig
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2; Motherboard: MSI MPG B850I Edge (mini-ITX)
GPU: GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition
CPU/RAM/Power/Cooling: Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000 PSU, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO
Host: GeForce Dwight (_MightyPotato); full parts list provided via PCPartPicker link
takeaways: High-end consumer GPUs like the RTX 5090 are being integrated into compact SFF chassis, highlighting thermal- and power-density considerations for tight form factors.
Use of a 1000W SFF PSU (Corsair SF1000) underscores elevated power delivery requirements even in small builds.
AIO cooling (Corsair Nautilus 240 RS) paired with a powerful GPU suggests active liquid cooling is used to manage thermals in compact systems.
Mini-ITX platforms (MSI MPG B850I Edge) with DDR5 memory can support high-performance components, relevant when evaluating hardware compatibility for dense deployments.
